fixture.seeded: seeded then torn down (catalog restored to pre-pilot state)
fixture.note: Dedicated finished-goods fixture; confirmed onHand 100 / committed 0 on seed.

proxyValidation.method: mast_orders create_test (online-order proxy for the UI POS write, which has no MCP tool)
proxyValidation.note: Proxy validates the before/after scorecard MECHANICS on real data. It exercises the online-order path + the protected-action handshake, NOT the true direct-pos channel or the inventory side-effect pipeline.

agent.role: oracle (no MCP POS-create tool — UI-only finding)

lens1.passAt1Note: A1/A2/A4/A5 pass exactly on the proxy; A3 (inventory) not exercised by create_test.
lens1.collateral: clean — only a 'test' channel bucket appeared; no real channel or other product changed; removed on teardown

assertions.name: A2 single channel +saleTotal
assertions.actual: new 'test' bucket +2500; all real channels unchanged
assertions.note: proxy lands in 'test'; a true POS sale would target pos/direct-pos

assertions.name: A3 inventory -qty
assertions.note: NOT EXERCISED: create_test skips the inventory side-effect pipeline; requires the real UI POS path

assertions.name: A5 no collateral change
assertions.actual: only 'test' channel added; fixture + all other products unchanged

ui.blockedOn: writable automated session (testmode is write-denied; no stored auth)

findings.title: Channel-key fragmentation in revenue byChannel
findings.detail: byChannel splits POS across 'pos' (460000) + 'direct-pos' (60000) and online across 'online' (202256) + 'Online Store' (13600). Total reconciles with P&L (not a money error) but per-channel reporting is unreliable. Root cause: revenue grouped on raw o.source/s.source with ad-hoc defaults and no canonical normalization (finance.js:541/551/603/621/1783/1795; channels.js:2466 default 'direct-pos'; dtc_online label 'Online Store' at channels.js:51/156). Flagged: task_f0d1c0e4.

findings.title: create_test orders are correctly segregated into a 'test' revenue channel
findings.detail: The proxy order landed in a new 'test' byChannel bucket, not in a real channel — good platform hygiene; test orders do not pollute real-channel revenue. (Positive finding.)

findings.title: mast_orders reads are protected (Lens-2 agent friction)
findings.detail: Every mast_orders action — including get and list — returns the two-call confirmation handshake, so order READS cost an agent double the calls/tokens. Reasonable for PII safety, but worth noting as an agent ease-of-use cost; consider a read-only unprotected projection for low-sensitivity order reads.
